data.title: India's Bharti Airtel drops after multiple block deals at discount
data.description:
** Shares of Bharti Airtel Ltd drop 2.3% to 1,879 rupees

** Stock biggest loser on blue-chip Nifty 50 index

** Over 73 million BRTI shares trade hands in 126 block deals, all at a discount to previous closing price of 1922.60 rupees - National Stock Exchange of India data

** News channel CNBC-TV18 on Thursday reported that promoter Indian Continent Investment plans to sell 0.8% stake it owns in co for $1 billion

** Over 78.5 million shares traded in stocks's second-most active session this year, 16.2x the 30-day average volume

** Analysts rate BRTI and Jio owner Reliance Industries "buy" on avg, while smaller rival Vodafone Idea rated "hold" - data compiled by LSEG

** BRTI stock up ~18% YTD, RELI up 14.3%, VODA down ~16%

data.title: Iron ore set for weekly gain as China's steel exports hit record high
data.description:
Iron ore futures edged lower on Friday but remain on track for a weekly gain, supported by China's record-high steel exports, robust mill margins, and low inventories.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) traded 0.57% lower at 787 yuan ($109.58) a metric ton, as of 0318 GMT.

Still, the contract is up 0.57% so far this week.

The benchmark September iron ore (SZZFU5) on the Singapore Exchange was 0.49% lower at $101.75 a ton, but up 1.9% so far this week.

Major miners are paying out their lowest dividends in years after reporting lower half-year earnings, seeking to retain cash for their major development projects.

BHP will spend up to $7.4 billion on its Jansen potash mine in Canada, while Rio Tinto expects to spend more than $13 billion in the next three years to develop new iron ore mines in Western Australia as existing reserves decline.

China's steel exports continued their rise in July, up 1.7% month-on-month, with the year-to-date tally at the highest level in records going back to 1990.

This comes despite the introduction of more trade barriers by countries worried that cheap Chinese steel is undercutting their domestic manufacturers.

Meanwhile, iron ore imports increased 2% year-on-year in July, well above average monthly imports for the year, as healthy mill margins and low steel inventories motivated mills to restock, said analysts from ANZ.

Broadly, ratings agency S&P Global has maintained China's long-term credit at A+, noting that its robust fiscal stimulus measures are expected to support the country's economic growth, even as it faces challenges from the property sector and tariff pressures.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE fell, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) down 0.82% and 1%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange mostly lost ground. Rebar dipped 0.8%, hot-rolled coil lost 0.87%, and wire rod (SWRcv1) eased 0.64%, while stainless steel edged up 0.08%.

($1 = 7.1820 Chinese yuan)

data.description:
• Texas Roadhouse Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.86​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.79. The mean expectation of twenty seven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.91 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.77 to $2.08 per share.

  • Revenue rose 12.7% to $1.51 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.50 billion.

  • Texas Roadhouse Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.86​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $124.09 million.

  • Texas Roadhouse Inc shares had fallen by 1.6% this quarter and gained 2.2% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.4% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, six analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," 18 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the restaurants & bars peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Texas Roadhouse Inc is $193.50, about 4.4% above its last closing price of $185.01

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 8 at 04:03 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: American Healthcare REIT Inc reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• American Healthcare REIT Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 14 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 1 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 10 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 8 cents to 13 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 7.5% to $542.50 million from a year ago; analysts expected $544.59 million.

  • American Healthcare REIT Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 6 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $9.91 million.

  • American Healthcare REIT Inc shares had risen by 7.9% this quarter and gained 39.5% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1.2% in the last three months.​

data.title: India's stock benchmarks inch lower at open, dragged down by IT, pharma
data.description:
India's equity benchmarks opened lower on Friday, weighed by a pullback in IT and pharma stocks, while hopes of a proposed U.S.-Russia presidential meeting easing pressure on global trade limited the downside.

The Nifty 50 fell 0.18% to 24,550.05 points and the BSE Sensex lost 0.24% to 80,425.23 as of 9:27 a.m. IST.

Eight of the 16 major sectors logged losses at open. Meanwhile, the broader small-caps and mid-caps traded flat.

U.S.-exposed IT stocks shed 0.5%, while the pharma index dropped 0.4%, after rising 0.9% and 0.8%, respectively, on Thursday.

The Nifty and Sensex fell nearly 1% each in intraday trade on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced an additional 25% tariff on Indian goods, taking the total to 50%, citing New Delhi's continued oil trade with Moscow.

However, markets rebounded after a Kremlin aide confirmed an upcoming meeting between Trump and his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, which analysts said could mark a turning point in the Russia-Ukraine conflict and prompt Washington to ease pressure on nations like India that import Russian oil.

Dow Jones Top Financial Services Headlines at 12 AM ET: Trump Shakes Up Wall Street With Orders on 401(k)s, 'Debanking' | Australia's ...
data.description:
Trump Shakes Up Wall Street With Orders on 401(k)s, 'Debanking'

A pair of executive orders President Trump signed Thursday show how he is beginning to reshape the world of banking and finance.

----

Australia's Iress in Takeover Talks With Private-Equity Firm Blackstone

Australian financial-technology provider Iress said it is in talks with Blackstone, having already considered an earlier proposal by the U.S. private-equity giant

----

Trump to Nominate Stephen Miran to Fed Board. What to Know About His Policy Views.

The move gives Trump his first opportunity to reshape the central bank's leadership in his second term.

----

Block Raises 2025 Outlook as Gross Profit Improves

The company behind payment systems Cash App and Square sees full-year gross profit, a key indicator for Block, increasing 14.4%, up from previous guidance of 12% growth.

----

Mortgage Rates Tumble to Early April Levels. Thank the Softer Jobs Report.

Mortgage rates move in parallel with the 10-year Treasury yield, which fell last week after signs of a weaker economy.

----

This Fed President Says Tariff Inflation Could Last Longer. Why He Still Expects Just 1 Rate Cut This Year.

Atlanta Federal Reserve President Raphael Bostic says shifting trade policy makes it difficult for businesses to plan for the long term.

----

Bank of England Cuts Rates After Tight Vote

The U.K. central bank lowered its key rate for the fifth time in a year as it seeks to balance a pickup in inflation against a cooling jobs market.

----

SoftBank Group Posts Quarterly Profit as AI Bet Pays Off

The Japanese technology investment company recorded a first-quarter net profit thanks partly to a recovery in its tech funds business.

----

Allianz's Earnings Beat Lifts Shares

Europe's largest insurer reported a 17% rise in second-quarter net profit and backed its guidance for the year.

----

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Allianz, Zurich Insurance and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

----

Brookfield Asset Management Sets Sights on 'In the Box' AI Investments

The firm sees opportunities in artificial-intelligence supply chains and hardware used in data centers.

----

TPG Sees 'Breakout Year' in Credit

The private-equity firm, which banked a $5.4 billion fundraising haul for credit in the second quarter, sees growing opportunity for bespoke deal financing.

----

Starboard Value Builds Over 9% Stake in Engineering-Materials Maker Rogers

The activist investor agitated at the company in 2023 and struck a settlement deal.
